goal: Plan and organize a combined extended family reunion and mom's milestone birthday
Incorporate potluck coordination for sides and desserts to balance options and reduce costs.
constraints: Consider Maple Street hall dishwasher availability and local composting rules when choosing dinnerware
Potluck coordination must avoid duplicates and ensure balanced options.
Eco-friendly decor and disposables prioritized; minimize waste using reusable, borrowed, or rental items where possible.

Insurance considerations: confirm COI requirements with Maple Street Hall; verify vendor liability coverage (and liquor liability if alcohol will be served); evaluate need for cancellation insurance if non-refundable deposits exist.
decisions: Budget allocation: Catering $1,400; Rentals $350; Entertainment $400; Contingency $300; Remaining $550
Partial potluck for sides and desserts approved to reduce costs.
Define recommended potluck categories and upper limits (e.g., 2 cold salads, 2 hot sides, 1–2 breads, 4–6 desserts, 2 kids' items).
Assign a coordinator to approve/redirect duplicates and manage sign-ups.
Use an online sign-up tool (Google Sheets/SignUpGenius/PerfectPotluck) to track contributions and avoid duplicates.
Set sign-up deadline 2 weeks before event.
Entertainment backup plan approved: curated playlist + MC/host + short live cameo; cameo cost target $50–$200 for a 15–30 minute performance; include option for a hobby DJ if budget permits.
Incorporate eco-friendly decor and disposables plan into event design (living centerpieces, linen rentals, digital programs, waste stations, and recyclable/compostable serveware).
Assess insurance needs with venue and vendors; obtain COIs and quotes for general liability, liquor liability (if applicable), and cancellation insurance.
Three 20% cut scenarios defined and ready for selection.
entities: labeling system for V and GF
DIY paper decor from recycled paper
balloon alternatives: paper lanterns, tissue pompoms, streamers
COI requirements; liability insurance; liquor liability; cancellation insurance
open: Obtain quotes from caterers with full line-item breakdown (food, labor, rentals, delivery, setup, cleanup, disposal, taxes, service fees).
Create and send a template email to request caterer details, sample contract, and event-day timeline.
Request confirmation on tasting policy, substitutions, and allergen handling in quotes.
Draft a brief caterer brief outlining Option A and Option B with portion counts, allergen notes, and rough per-guest price estimates.
Draft eco-friendly decor plan and cost estimate
Create waste-station signage and volunteer assignment
Draft an eco-friendly invite blurb or printable sign (carpooling, bring-your-mug, etc.)
Define RSVP deadline to coordinate with caterer (final headcount due 7 days before event).
Draft RSVP wording templates for digital invitations (include deadline and dietary notes) and for mailed invitations.
Plan follow-up reminders for non-respondents (e.g., first reminder 5–7 days after invitation; final reminder 2–3 days before deadline).
Add RSVP deadline to invitation materials (digital and paper) and coordinate with caterer sign-off.
Ask Maple Street Hall whether a COI naming them as additional insured is required.
Obtain quotes for general liability and cancellation insurance for the event; include liquor liability if applicable.
Request COIs from caterer, band, rentals; verify coverage is sufficient to meet venue requirements.
Choose one of the three 20% cut scenarios to implement and document rationale.
Notify vendors (caterer, entertainment, rentals, photographer) of selected plan and request revised quotes.
Obtain updated quotes reflecting proposed cut plan.
Adjust budget allocations to reflect chosen plan; ensure total aligns with $3,000 budget.
Communicate menu changes to guests if needed (e.g., more potluck contributions).
Review contingency amount to ensure minimal risk; consider adjustments to keep small contingency.
Coordinate with Maple Street Hall regarding updated COI or contract terms after changes.
